authorization.scope: WP-10B entry and implementation, current-source D-009 verification, trusted local filesystem and rootless-VM durability work, product cutover, predecessor deletion, and product-open validation

inherited_contracts.rule: canonical project state, revision, high-water, and scientific identity remain authoritative
WP-10B consumes the frozen subsystem APIs and removes the project-v15 bridge at its expiry gate
D-009 identity primitives may be consumed, but target storage remains off-product until WP-10C

outcome: Implement one mirante4d-project-store authority for immutable objects, complete generations, refs, leases, autosave, recovery, Save As, and conservative compaction.
Add a bounded current-source scientific verification operation and cache, then promote the whole live source session to a verified catalog before project attach, open, or save.
Switch the product atomically to the new store and delete the private project-v15 bridge, CurrentProjectRuntime, their tests, and every legacy project reader or alternate path.
Prove canonical bytes independently, hostile process/filesystem behavior locally, and the real viewer save/edit/recover/reopen workflow on the accepted Linux display targets.
entry_audit_facts: The current source catalog is always unverified and production has no D-009 verification completion; a test-only admission helper is not a product seam.
Identity promotion must replace or restart the source runtime with a verified catalog because verified and unverified semantic resource keys are intentionally different.
The project-v15 bridge is the only current project I/O path and has no autosave, Save As, OS lease, expected-parent conflict, object store, recovery refs, or compaction.
The canonical project model already owns the required dataset-reference split, project revision, revision high-water, and generation projection.

dependency_contract.temporary_identity_edge: mirante4d-data may add mirante4d-identity only for the approved current-source verification bridge
dependency_contract.rule: a required change to identity, dataset, dataset-runtime, current format, or WP-10A storage APIs stops the package for corrective review

public_api_boundary.checkpoint_b1: freeze at most sixteen cohesive root exports covering path, limits/configuration, generation/object identity, open mode/session, actor command/completion, recovery candidate, diagnostics, and typed fault
public_api_boundary.forbidden: renderer, GPU, UI, worker-thread handle, current-format DTO, storage DTO, arbitrary internal path, owning unbounded byte vector, or segmentation type
public_api_boundary.persistence_projection: project-model remains serialization-free; project-store owns the closed wire DTO and maps only the accepted ProjectGenerationProjection

store_limits.rule: limit exhaustion is typed and publishes no ref; large artifacts stream in fixed buffers and never create one object per row, timepoint, voxel, or dataset brick

persistence_contract.records: closed canonical project envelope, binary checksummed refs, canonical JSON generations, artifact page manifests, and exact-byte objects
persistence_contract.commit_order: lease and expected-parent check; immutable objects; complete generation; independent recovery ref; atomic head replacement; refs-directory sync
persistence_contract.indeterminate_rule: failed final durability keeps the revision dirty, suspends writes, and requires close/reopen recovery
persistence_contract.locking: shared whole-session maintenance flock followed by writable-session writer flock; lock-file presence is never authority
persistence_contract.autosave: same generation format, separate head/recovery refs, explicit base manual generation, one coalesced queued request
persistence_contract.compaction: unknown files, corrupt graphs, active leases, or an over-cap recovery review block deletion; unreachable content moves to synced trash before separate purge
persistence_contract.filesystem_policy: write support only for an evidence-qualified Linux filesystem and exact mount-option tuple; every other filesystem is read-only

current_source_identity_contract.algorithm: stream canonical base-scale values and validity through existing D-009 ScientificDatasetHasher primitives while validating the full current encoded-object checksum inventory
current_source_identity_contract.bounds: at most two runtime-accounted resource leases, one 1-MiB streaming buffer, and a 64-MiB checksum-inventory cache; bounded progress/completion queues; cancellation checked between resources and stream blocks
current_source_identity_contract.cache: machine-local and non-portable; reuse requires an exact manifest/checksum inventory, normalized object closure, sizes, and encoded-object digests; any drift invalidates it
current_source_identity_contract.promotion: typed application completion replaces the current source/runtime generation with a verified catalog before attaching or restoring a project
current_source_identity_contract.failure: cancel, mismatch, mutation, or unverifiable cache leaves the workspace unbound and project I/O unavailable

fixtures.authority: independent Python producer and dependency-isolated read-only validator
fixtures.scope: canonical envelope/ref/generation/page/object bytes and digests, two manual generations with object reuse, autosave/recovery/divergence, exact recovery candidates, and typed corrupt mutations
named before/after failpoints, short writes, ENOSPC, permissions, corruption, symlink races, concurrent writers, stale parents, interrupted compaction, relocation, and cross-device Save As
real product current-source verification plus save, edit-during-save, autosave recovery, reopen, and Save As without source mutation

durability_harness.kvm: /dev/kvm is writable by the designated user
durability_harness.filesystem: ext4 created by e2fsprogs 1.47.0; exact observed features and mount options are recorded in evidence
durability_harness.power_cut: attach each ext4 image through rootless nbdkit cache=writeback, kill QEMU and nbdkit at every authoritative transition in the frozen inventory, then restart from the durable backing images and validate in a fresh guest process; explicit guest flushes must reach the backing image
durability_harness.cross_device: the two independent ext4 virtio disks provide the positive copy-and-rehash Save As case
durability_harness.no_large_dataset: all large-size boundaries use checked arithmetic or bounded streams; no TiB dataset is allocated or simulated on disk

implementation_checkpoints: B1: accepted entry, exact project-store contract, crate boundary, canonical wire schemas, promoted T1-project fixture, independent validator, and architecture/verification ownership

B2: descriptor-relative transactional core, immutable objects/generations/refs, leases, manual save/Save As, autosave/recovery, compaction/trash, actor, exhaustive failpoints, fresh-process kills, and rootless-VM durability evidence; still off-product

B3: bounded current-source D-009 verification/cache, typed application completion, atomic verified-catalog runtime replacement, and project-store application service integration; project-v15 remains the sole product route until B4
B4: atomic product save/open/autosave/recovery cutover, complete project-v15 and CurrentProjectRuntime deletion, real viewer validation, clean protected-main evidence, and WP-10B exit

verification.product: real mapped viewer on the public T2 current package at 1280x720 and 1920x1080: verify identity, save, edit while saving, autosave, kill, recover, reopen, Save As, and source nonmutation
verification.report: bind commit/tree/clean state, fixture and executable digests, filesystem/kernel/mount/QEMU facts, transition and kill matrix, byte/object/work counters, product display facts, source digests, results, and all failures without private paths

mandatory_deletions_at_b4: every mirante4d-project-v15 DTO, reader, writer, fixture, test, selector, and runtime string outside immutable historical contracts
all sixteen predecessor bridge tests and their live verification dispositions
invariants: Only project-store owns project paths, canonical persistence bytes, filesystem mutation, OS leases, refs, generations, objects, autosave/recovery, and compaction.
Only application owns live revision/history movement and decides whether an exact completed revision becomes saved.
A ref never names staging or an incomplete object closure; before the durable head switch the old generation remains authoritative.
No v13, v14, or v15 reader, converter, migration shim, feature flag, fallback, or alternate product project path survives B4.
Dataset locators remain hints; only a verified D-009 scientific identity may bind a project, and source bytes are never mutated.
Unknown filesystems, unknown files, corrupt graphs, active maintenance leases, and unreviewable recovery sets fail closed.
All large bytes, queues, scans, files, descriptors, processes, and VM artifacts remain inside the declared limits and are cancellable where publication safety permits.
Both new store code and bridge additions forbid unsafe code.
non_goals: WP-10A target-storage product activation, current dataset-format deletion, import, analysis, rendering, settings, segmentation, public-data publication, or release hardening
Stable project-format compatibility, a generic project reader, cloud/network/FUSE write support, automatic project merge, silent recovery, or automatic deletion of non-regenerable data
Large real microscopy data, TiB disk images, 4K display qualification, paid CI, or a public self-hosted runner
stop_conditions: Any inherited model, identity, dataset, dataset-runtime, format, or WP-10A storage API must change rather than being consumed through the accepted bridge boundaries.
Current-source verification cannot validate the full checksum inventory, remain bounded/cancellable, or atomically replace the live runtime with a verified catalog.
Canonical fixture facts depend on the production serializer/reader, or the independent validator cannot reject the declared corruptions.
Any ref can expose a partial closure, an existing digest can be accepted without exact validation, or a capacity/failure path changes authority.
The rootless VM cannot discard guest-unpersisted writes, positive cross-device Save As cannot use two filesystems, or the 900-second/640-MiB harness ceilings are exceeded.
A product cutover requires retaining project-v15, adding a compatibility route, mutating source data, or skipping real mapped-viewer save/recovery/reopen validation.

rollback.b1_b2_b3: revert the failed checkpoint while the project-v15 product route remains sole and delete non-authoritative generated output; never add a fallback
rollback.b4: the authority flip and predecessor deletion are one atomic protected-main checkpoint; preserve verified backups of test projects before merge and fix forward afterward
rollback.format: the new project store remains experimental and disposable development projects are regenerated; no converter is implied
